w:r.w:t: Mobile & Tablet ONLY — Desktop/PC is untouched
Everything in this document is wrapped inside @media (max-width: 1024px). Zero changes are made to the desktop layout. PC users see the existing GBA shell exactly as it is.

The current PokePlay implementation works well on desktop — the GBA shell, canvas, and keyboard controls are all correctly positioned. However on mobile and tablet devices, the on-screen control buttons are visually distorted, have no spatial relationship to each other, and do not reflect the physical layout of a real Game Boy Advance console.
Canvas is letterboxed top and bottom — wasted screen real estate
D-pad, A/B, and meta buttons are independent with no container
Button positions do not match muscle memory of real GBA layout
Buttons sized in px — too small on some phones, too large on others
Four individual buttons — no diagonal input support
The desktop and PC layout is perfect and will not be touched. All fixes in this document are exclusively scoped inside @media (max-width: 1024px). No existing desktop CSS or JS is modified.

The fundamental fix is to stop thinking of the layout as "screen with floating buttons" and instead treat the entire mobile viewport as two locked, non-overlapping zones with a hard boundary between them.
│ CONTROL ZONE │ ← fixed height, never shrinks
The control zone height is fixed and calculated first. The screen zone gets whatever viewport height remains. This guarantees controls are never squished or cut off regardless of phone model.
for the control zone. On mobile, the browser address bar collapses and expands, causing
is calculated against the smallest possible viewport (address bar fully visible) — this guarantees the control zone never gets cropped when the browser chrome changes.
Every layout decision must be grounded in the physical GBA layout. This is what players' muscle memory already knows — the implementation must match it exactly.
Rule: A/B always bottom-right (A higher-right, B lower-left of pair).
Rule: L/R shoulder buttons always top edge, full width.
Rule: SELECT/START always center, between D-pad and A/B.
Inside the control zone, use a
CSS Grid with 3 columns and 2 rows
. This is the correct abstraction — not absolute positioning, not flexbox guessing.
There is no single mobile layout. Three distinct layouts are required, each with its own media query.
Stacked vertically — screen top, controls bottom
This is the hardest layout. The screen and controls sit
— the only way to maximise screen size in landscape without the controls covering the game.
/* L/R span full width of control zone */
Vertical (same as portrait phone, more space)
Scaled up 1.3× vs phone via CSS custom properties
Tablet users have more precision than phone users. Bigger buttons with more spacing between them — do not just scale everything up proportionally, also increase inter-button gaps.
All button dimensions must be expressed as
values — a minimum (accessibility floor), a preferred viewport-relative value, and a maximum (prevents buttons becoming absurdly large on wide tablets).
Never set any interactive button below 44×44px. This is the Apple Human Interface Guideline minimum touch target. Anything smaller is unreliable to tap reliably with a finger.
Use CSS custom properties to manage all sizes in one place:
/* Tablet overrides — only inside media query */
The current implementation uses four separate buttons for the D-pad. This must be replaced with a
that calculates direction from touch position relative to the center point. This gives:
— pressing between up and right fires both simultaneously, essential for GBA games
— one event listener instead of four
— matches how real hardware D-pads work
Create a plus/cross shape using a 3×3 CSS Grid with the four corner cells hidden:
display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
/* Hide the four corners — makes it a cross shape */
/* Center cell — inert, just fills the gap */
const deadzone = rect.width * 0.15; // 15% deadzone in center
// Diagonal support: fire both axes if both exceed threshold
On the physical GBA, A and B are not side by side horizontally — they are
. A is upper-right, B is lower-left. This is deliberate hardware ergonomics. The implementation must match.
The full restructured HTML for the mobile layout. The key change is separating
as proper siblings, with the control zone containing a structured grid.
<!-- ZONE 2: Controls (mobile/tablet only — hidden on desktop) -->
All mobile CSS is wrapped in a single media query guard. Nothing leaks to desktop.
Desktop/PC: ZERO changes below this breakpoint boundary.
/* Hide the desktop GBA shell on mobile */
/* All portrait phone, landscape phone, tablet CSS goes here */
/* Desktop — show shell, hide mobile controls */
All the CSS in Section 4, 5, 6, 7 lives inside @media (max-width: 1024px). The desktop experience at > 1024px is guaranteed untouched.
1 focused day. Do steps in order — each builds on the previous.
all existing absolute-positioned control buttons from the current layout
Build the control zone inner HTML
exactly as shown in Section 8 — shoulder-row, dpad-area, meta-area, ab-area
Step 3 — Portrait Phone Layout CSS (45 min)
Style D-pad cross shape via CSS grid 3×3
Style A/B with diagonal offset positioning
Style L/R shoulder buttons spanning full width
Step 4 — Landscape Phone Layout CSS (30 min)
Adjust control zone grid for side-by-side layout
Scale up button sizes via custom property overrides
Increase inter-button gaps for tablet finger precision
Step 6 — D-Pad Unified JS Handler (30 min)
listeners from the old D-pad buttons
Test diagonal inputs — walking diagonally in Fire Red should work smoothly
Step 7 — Test on Real Devices (1 hour)
Controls not clipped, buttons reachable with thumbs
Proportions feel correct, no wasted space
Desktop layout completely unchanged — no regression
Use for quick iteration before real device testing
Buttons drift at different screen sizes
Use CSS Grid columns — buttons always in correct relative positions
Too small on large phones, too big on small ones
Using vh for control zone height
Jumps when browser address bar shows/hides
Single div with pointermove + direction math from center
A and B in a horizontal line
Wrong — breaks muscle memory from real GBA
Diagonal offset: A upper-right, B lower-left
Every mobile CSS line must be inside @media (max-width: 1024px)
Browser tries to scroll while player presses D-pad
Pointer events stop firing if finger drifts off element
A = top-right, B = bottom-left of cluster
